text: Build the hackathon extension: visible aim-to-commit provenance with dependency context
description: Create one coherent, judge-visible capability after 13 July: from an aim, show implementation evidence from Git commits (existing realized-commit signal), the aim's supporting dependencies, and linked-repo context. The experience should answer: what did we intend, what did it depend on, and what code actually realized it? Reuse existing .bowman persistence, linked-repo model, and commit-reference machinery. Keep scope to a polished vertical slice with tests; do not attempt a platform rewrite.

status.comment: Candidate core extension; implement only after go/no-go.
